calendar.matters:
Notice And Motion For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor
Off Calendar Mar-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff National Credit Acceptance Inc.'s motion for an assignment and restraining order is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on February 13, 2008 and served by mail. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor
Off Calendar Jan-14-2008 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion & Mtn For Award Of Costs Re Asset Investigation For Enforcement Of Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-06-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion for costs relating to the enforcement of judgment is denied without prejudice. The declaration submitted in support of the motion is not competent evidence of the costs incurred for investigation and attorney's fees. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
